titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Richard Benz Collection on David Wojnarowicz MSS.660
titlestmt.author: Rachel Searcy

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-05-14 19:42:30 UTC.

did.unittitle: Richard Benz Collection on David Wojnarowicz
did.abstract: Richard Benz is a poet who worked at the New York City bookshop Bookmasters with David Wojnarowicz. The Richard Benz Collection on David Wojnarowicz (dated 1975-1976) consists of two postcards; two newsletters, one inscribed to Benz by Wojnarowicz and one with an accompanying letter; and four typewritten poems, one signed by Wojnarowicz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Richard Benz Collection on David Wojnarowicz (dated 1975-1976) consists of two postcards; two newsletters, one inscribed to Benz by Wojnarowicz and one with an accompanying letter; and four typewritten poems, one signed by Wojnarowicz.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: This collection is protected by copyright and/or related rights. You are free to use materials in the collection in any way that is permitted by the copyright and related rights legislation that applies to your use. For other uses you need to obtain permission from the rights-holder(s).

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Donated by Richard Benz in May 2026; the accession number associated with this gift is 2026.024.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date; Richard Benz Collection on David Wojnarowicz; MSS 660; box number; folder number or item identifier; Fales Library and Special Collections, New York University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: At the time of accessioning materials were rehoused in archival folders and boxes, described on the collection-level, and inventoried at the folder-level.

bioghist.head: Biographical Note
bioghist.p: Richard Benz is a poet whose work appears in Life Without Parole: Being a One-Shot Anthology of Poetry published by the Poetry Project of St. Mark's Church. He worked at the New York City bookshop Bookmasters with David Wojnarowicz.
